definition: Lasting for only a short time; transitory.
example: The ephemeral beauty of cherry blossoms makes them all the more precious.
text: ephemeral (adjective): lasting for only a short time; transitory.

definition: Having a ready insight into things; shrewd and discerning.
example: The perspicacious detective noticed the small inconsistency in the alibi at once.
text: perspicacious (adjective): having a ready insight into things; shrewd and discerning.

definition: Optimistic or positive, especially in a difficult situation.
example: Despite the setbacks, she remained sanguine about the project's outcome.
text: sanguine (adjective): optimistic or positive, especially in a difficult situation.

definition: To make obscure, unclear, or hard to understand.
example: The lawyer used technical jargon to obfuscate the simple facts of the case.
text: obfuscate (verb): to make obscure, unclear, or hard to understand.

definition: Using very few words; brief and to the point.
example: Her laconic reply — 'No.' — ended the debate instantly.
text: laconic (adjective): using very few words; brief and to the point.

definition: To make something bad or unsatisfactory better.
example: The new policy was designed to ameliorate the harsh working conditions in the factory.
text: ameliorate (verb): to make something bad or unsatisfactory better.

definition: Very weak or slight; lacking substance or solidity.
example: The connection between the two events was tenuous at best.
text: tenuous (adjective): very weak or slight; lacking substance or solidity.

definition: Very attentive to accuracy, detail, and cleanliness.
example: He was fastidious about his appearance, never leaving the house with a wrinkle in his shirt.
text: fastidious (adjective): very attentive to accuracy, detail, and cleanliness.

definition: Having an obstinately uncooperative attitude toward authority.
example: The recalcitrant student refused to follow the classroom rules despite repeated warnings.
text: recalcitrant (adjective): having an obstinately uncooperative attitude toward authority.

definition: Sweet or musical; pleasantly smooth in sound.
example: Her mellifluous voice made even the dullest announcements sound like poetry.
text: mellifluous (adjective): sweet or musical; pleasantly smooth in sound.

definition: To use ambiguous or unclear language in order to avoid committing oneself.
example: When asked about the budget cuts, the politician equivocated rather than giving a straight answer.
text: equivocate (verb): to use ambiguous language to avoid committing oneself.

definition: Second to last in a series or sequence.
example: The penultimate chapter set up a shocking twist that paid off in the finale.
text: penultimate (adjective): second to last in a series or sequence.

definition: A typical example or pattern; a model that forms the basis of a discipline or theory.
example: The discovery of DNA shifted the paradigm of biological science.
text: paradigm (noun): a typical example or pattern; a model that forms the basis of a discipline.

definition: The occurrence and development of events by chance in a happy or beneficial way.
example: Finding her dream apartment while looking for a coffee shop was pure serendipity.
text: serendipity (noun): the occurrence of fortunate events by chance.

definition: Present, appearing, or found everywhere.
example: Smartphones have become so ubiquitous that it is rare to see someone without one.
text: ubiquitous (adjective): present, appearing, or found everywhere.

definition: A person who acts obsequiously toward influential people in order to gain advantage.
example: The boardroom was full of sycophants who agreed with every word the CEO said.
text: sycophant (noun): a person who flatters influential people to gain advantage.

definition: Tending to talk a great deal; chatty.
example: His loquacious nature made every five-minute story into a thirty-minute saga.
text: loquacious (adjective): tending to talk a great deal; chatty.

definition: Reserved or uncommunicative in speech; saying little.
example: The taciturn farmer answered every question with a nod or a single word.
text: taciturn (adjective): reserved in speech; saying little.

definition: Clear, logical, and convincing.
example: She made a cogent argument that convinced even the most skeptical members of the committee.
text: cogent (adjective): clear, logical, and convincing.

definition: A feeling of listlessness and dissatisfaction arising from a lack of occupation or excitement.
example: After weeks of vacation with nothing to do, he was overcome by ennui.
text: ennui (noun): listlessness and dissatisfaction from a lack of occupation or excitement.

definition: Generous or forgiving, especially toward a rival or less powerful person.
example: The champion was magnanimous in victory, praising her opponent's skill.
text: magnanimous (adjective): generous or forgiving, especially toward a rival.

definition: Deceitful and untrustworthy; guilty of betrayal.
example: The perfidious advisor leaked state secrets to the enemy while posing as a loyal confidant.
text: perfidious (adjective): deceitful and untrustworthy; guilty of betrayal.

definition: Ostentatiously rich and luxurious.
example: The hotel lobby was opulent, with marble floors, gold fixtures, and crystal chandeliers.
text: opulent (adjective): ostentatiously rich and luxurious.

definition: Dealing with things sensibly and realistically, based on practical rather than theoretical considerations.
example: Rather than chasing perfection, the engineer took a pragmatic approach and shipped a working solution.
text: pragmatic (adjective): dealing with things sensibly based on practical considerations.

definition: Experienced or felt in the imagination through the feelings or actions of another person.
example: She got vicarious thrills from reading adventure novels while staying cozy at home.
text: vicarious (adjective): experienced through the feelings or actions of another person.
